1→class SandboxManager
     2→  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
     3→  DEFAULT_IMAGE = "ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest"
     4→  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"
     5→
     6→  class Error < StandardError; end
     7→
     8→  def create(user:, name:, image: DEFAULT_IMAGE, persistent: false, tailscale: false, mount_home: false, data_path: nil, temporary: false)
     9→    # Build sandbox record (not saved yet)
    10→    sandbox = user.sandboxes.build(
    11→      name: name,
    12→      image: image,
    13→      status: "pending",
    14→      persistent_volume: persistent,
    15→      mount_home: mount_home,
    16→      data_path: data_path,
    17→      temporary: temporary
    18→    )
    19→
    20→    if persistent
    21→      sandbox.volume_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/sandboxes/#{sandbox.full_name}/vol"
    22→    end
    23→
    24→    # Validate before doing expensive operations
    25→    sandbox.validate!
    26→
    27→    # Create directories FIRST (can fail fast before saving record)
    28→    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
    29→
    30→    # Now safe to save
    31→    sandbox.save!
    32→
    33→    # Pull image
    34→    ensure_image(image)
    35→
    36→    # Create and start container
    37→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
    38→
    39→    # Connect to Tailscale if requested
    40→    if (tailscale || user.tailscale_auto_connect?) && user.tailscale_enabled?
    41→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
    42→    end
    43→
    44→    sandbox
    45→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    46→    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    47→    raise Error, "Failed to create container: #{e.message}"
    48→  rescue => e
    49→    # If anything fails before save, no DB record is created
    50→    # If anything fails after save, mark as destroyed
    51→    sandbox&.update(status: "destroyed") if sandbox&.persisted?
    52→    raise Error, e.message
    53→  end
    54→
    55→  # Public method for job usage
    56→  def create_container_and_start(sandbox:, user:)
    57→    container = Docker::Container.create(
    58→      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
    59→      "Image" => sandbox.image,
    60→      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
    61→      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
    62→      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.sandbox" => "true" },
    63→      "HostConfig" => {
    64→        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
    65→        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
    66→        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
    67→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
    68→      },
    69→      "NetworkingConfig" => {
    70→        "EndpointsConfig" => { NETWORK_NAME => {} }
    71→      }
    72→    )
    73→
    74→    container.start
    75→    container.refresh!
    76→    unless container.json.dig("State", "Running")
    77→      state_error = container.json.dig("State", "Error").presence || container.json.dig("State", "Status")
    78→      container.stop rescue nil
    79→      container.delete(force: true) rescue nil
    80→      raise Error, "Container failed to start: #{state_error}"
    81→    end
    82→    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, status: "running")
    83→
    84→    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately (no wait on first open).
    85→    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
    86→    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?
    87→  end
    88→
    89→  # Public method for job usage
    90→  def ensure_image(image)
    91→    Docker::Image.get(image)
    92→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
    93→    raise Error, "Snapshot image #{image} not found locally (snapshots are never pulled from a registry)" if image.start_with?("sc-snap-")
    94→    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => image)
    95→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    96→    raise Error, "Failed to pull image #{image}: #{e.message}"
    97→  end
    98→
    99→  # Public method for job usage
   100→  def ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   101→    # Create BTRFS subvolume for user directory if on BTRFS
   102→    BtrfsHelper.create_user_subvolume(user.name)
   103→
   104→    # Ensure user base directory is writable (may be root-owned from previous install)
   105→    ensure_dir("#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}")
   106→
   107→    if sandbox.mount_home
   108→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   109→      ensure_dir(dir)
   110→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   111→    end
   112→    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   113→      # Create BTRFS subvolume for data directory if on BTRFS
   114→      BtrfsHelper.create_user_data_subvolume(user.name, sandbox.data_path)
   115→
   116→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   117→      ensure_dir(dir)
   118→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   119→    end
   120→    if sandbox.persistent_volume && sandbox.volume_path
   121→      ensure_dir(sandbox.volume_path)
   122→      prepare_bind_mount(sandbox.volume_path)
   123→    end
   124→    # Chrome profile persistence: mount .config/google-chrome separately if not mounting full home
   125→    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   126→      dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   127→      ensure_dir(dir)
   128→      prepare_bind_mount(dir)
   129→    end
   130→  rescue Errno::EACCES, Errno::ENOENT => e
   131→    raise Error, "Failed to create mount directories: #{e.message}"
   132→  end
   133→
   134→  def destroy(sandbox:, keep_volume: false, archive: false)
   135→    begin
   136→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   137→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   138→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   139→    end
   140→
   141→    begin
   142→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   143→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   144→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   145→    end
   146→
   147→    RouteManager.new.remove_all_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   148→
   149→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   150→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   151→    end
   152→
   153→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   154→      begin
   155→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   156→        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   157→        container.delete(force: true)
   158→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   159→        # Container already gone
   160→      end
   161→    end
   162→
   163→    if archive
   164→      # Soft-delete: keep volume on disk, mark as archived.
   165→      # Rename to free the original name for reuse.
   166→      archived_name = "#{Time.current.strftime('%Y%m%d%H%M%S')}-#{sandbox.name}"
   167→      sandbox.update!(status: "archived", container_id: nil, archived_at: Time.current, name: archived_name)
   168→    else
   169→      unless keep_volume
   170→        FileUtils.rm_rf(sandbox.volume_path) if sandbox.volume_path.present?
   171→      end
   172→      sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   173→    end
   174→  end
   175→
   176→  # Restore an archived sandbox: recreate the container from the existing volume.
   177→  # The container is started immediately; status is set to "running".
   178→  def restore_from_archive(sandbox:)
   179→    raise Error, "Sandbox is not archived" unless sandbox.status == "archived"
   180→
   181→    user = sandbox.user
   182→
   183→    ensure_image(sandbox.image)
   184→    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   185→
   186→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   187→    # Strip the timestamp prefix added during archival (e.g. "20260307123456-mybox" → "mybox")
   188→    original_name = sandbox.name.sub(/\A\d{14}-/, "")
   189→    sandbox.update!(archived_at: nil, name: original_name)
   190→
   191→    if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
   192→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   193→    end
   194→
   195→    sandbox
   196→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   197→    raise Error, "Failed to restore archived sandbox: #{e.message}"
   198→  end
   199→
   200→  def start(sandbox:)
   201→    raise Error, "Sandbox is destroyed" if sandbox.status == "destroyed"
   202→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "running"
   203→
   204→    user = sandbox.user
   205→
   206→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   207→      begin
   208→        old = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   209→        old.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   210→        old.delete(force: true)
   211→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   212→        # already gone
   213→      end
   214→    end
   215→
   216→    # Reset bind-mount directory permissions before starting the new container.
   217→    # After a previous Sysbox run, dirs may be owned by a remapped UID that
   218→    # Rails can't chmod — ensure_mount_dirs uses sudo to reset to 777.
   219→    ensure_mount_dirs(user, sandbox)
   220→
   221→    create_container_and_start(sandbox: sandbox, user: user)
   222→
   223→    TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_enabled?
   224→    RouteManager.new.reconnect_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   225→
   226→    sandbox
   227→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   228→    sandbox.update!(status: "destroyed", container_id: nil)
   229→    raise Error, "Container not found — sandbox must be recreated"
   230→  end
   231→
   232→  def stop(sandbox:)
   233→    return sandbox if sandbox.status == "stopped"
   234→
   235→    begin
   236→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   237→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   238→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   239→    end
   240→
   241→    begin
   242→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   243→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   244→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   245→    end
   246→
   247→    RouteManager.new.suspend_routes(sandbox: sandbox) if sandbox.routed?
   248→
   249→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   250→      begin
   251→        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   252→        container.stop(t: 10)
   253→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   254→        # already gone
   255→      end
   256→    end
   257→
   258→    sandbox.update!(status: "stopped")
   259→    sandbox
   260→  end
   261→
   262→  SERVICES = %w[docker vnc].freeze
   263→
   264→  def service_start(sandbox:, service:)
   265→    raise Error, "Unknown service: #{service}" unless SERVICES.include?(service)
   266→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   267→
   268→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   269→    user = sandbox.user.name
   270→
   271→    case service
   272→    when "docker"
   273→      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pgrep -x dockerd > /dev/null && echo already_running && exit 0; dockerd --storage-driver=overlay2 --mtu=$(ip link show eth0 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'mtu \\K[0-9]+' || echo 1500) &>/var/log/dockerd.log & echo started" ])
   274→    when "vnc"
   275→      geometry = sandbox.vnc_geometry || "1280x900"
   276→      depth = sandbox.vnc_depth || 24
   277→      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pgrep -x Xvnc > /dev/null && echo already_running && exit 0; su -s /bin/bash #{user} -c 'Xvnc :99 -rfbport 5900 -SecurityTypes None -AlwaysShared -geometry #{geometry} -depth #{depth} &>/var/log/xvnc.log &' && su -s /bin/bash #{user} -c 'DISPLAY=:99 openbox &>/var/log/openbox.log &' && websockify -addr :6080 -target localhost:5900 -url /websockify &>/var/log/websockify.log & echo started" ])
   278→    end
   279→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   280→    raise Error, "Container not found"
   281→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   282→    raise Error, "Failed to start #{service}: #{e.message}"
   283→  end
   284→
   285→  def service_stop(sandbox:, service:)
   286→    raise Error, "Unknown service: #{service}" unless SERVICES.include?(service)
   287→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   288→
   289→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   290→
   291→    case service
   292→    when "docker"
   293→      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pkill -x dockerd 2>/dev/null; pkill -x containerd 2>/dev/null; echo stopped" ])
   294→    when "vnc"
   295→      container.exec([ "bash", "-c", "pkill -x websockify 2>/dev/null; pkill -x openbox 2>/dev/null; pkill -x Xvnc 2>/dev/null; echo stopped" ])
   296→    end
   297→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   298→    raise Error, "Container not found"
   299→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   300→    raise Error, "Failed to stop #{service}: #{e.message}"
   301→  end
   302→
   303→  def logs(sandbox:, tail: 200, timestamps: false)
   304→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   305→
   306→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   307→    container.logs(stdout: true, stderr: true, follow: false, tail: tail, timestamps: timestamps)
   308→      .force_encoding("UTF-8").encode("UTF-8", invalid: :replace, undef: :replace, replace: "")
   309→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   310→    raise Error, "Container not found"
   311→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   312→    raise Error, "Failed to fetch logs: #{e.message}"
   313→  end
   314→
   315→  def status(sandbox:)
   316→    return { state: "destroyed" } if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   317→
   318→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   319→    info = container.json
   320→    {
   321→      state: info.dig("State", "Status"),
   322→      running: info.dig("State", "Running"),
   323→      started_at: info.dig("State", "StartedAt"),
   324→      pid: info.dig("State", "Pid")
   325→    }
   326→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   327→    { state: "not_found" }
   328→  end
   329→
   330→  # Create a composite snapshot (Docker image + optional BTRFS layers).
   331→  # Returns a Snapshot ActiveRecord object.
   332→  #
   333→  # layers: array of "container", "home", "data", "workspace"
   334→  #   nil means "all available" based on sandbox config
   335→  def create_snapshot(sandbox:, name:, label: nil, layers: nil, data_subdir: nil)
   336→    raise Error, "Sandbox has no running container" if sandbox.container_id.blank?
   337→
   338→    user = sandbox.user
   339→    name = name.presence || Date.today.iso8601
   340→    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s) || %w[container home data workspace]
   341→
   342→    snap = Snapshot.new(
   343→      user: user,
   344→      name: name,
   345→      label: label,
   346→      source_sandbox: sandbox.name,
   347→      data_subdir: data_subdir
   348→    )
   349→
   350→    # ── Container layer ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   351→    if requested_layers.include?("container")
   352→      repo = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   353→      container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   354→      image = container.commit(
   355→        repo: repo,
   356→        tag: name,
   357→        comment: "sandbox:#{sandbox.name}"
   358→      )
   359→      snap.docker_image = "#{repo}:#{name}"
   360→      snap.docker_size  = image.info["Size"]
   361→    end
   362→
   363→    # ── Home layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   364→    if requested_layers.include?("home") && sandbox.mount_home? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   365→      home_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   366→      home_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/home"
   367→      if Dir.exist?(home_src)
   368→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(home_src, home_dest)
   369→        snap.home_snapshot = home_dest
   370→        snap.home_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(home_dest)
   371→      end
   372→    end
   373→
   374→    # ── Data layer (BTRFS only) ───────────────────────────────────────────────
   375→    if requested_layers.include?("data") && sandbox.data_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   376→      if data_subdir.present?
   377→        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   378→      else
   379→        data_src  = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   380→      end
   381→      data_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/data"
   382→      if Dir.exist?(data_src)
   383→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(data_src, data_dest)
   384→        snap.data_snapshot = data_dest
   385→        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(data_dest)
   386→      end
   387→    end
   388→
   389→    # ── Workspace layer (BTRFS only) ─────────────────────────────────────────
   390→    if requested_layers.include?("workspace") && sandbox.persistent_volume? && sandbox.volume_path.present? && BtrfsHelper.btrfs?
   391→      workspace_src  = sandbox.volume_path
   392→      workspace_dest = "#{DATA_DIR}/snapshots/#{user.name}/#{name}/workspace"
   393→      if Dir.exist?(workspace_src)
   394→        BtrfsHelper.snapshot_subvolume(workspace_src, workspace_dest)
   395→        # Store workspace alongside data_snapshot when no data layer taken
   396→        snap.data_snapshot ||= workspace_dest
   397→        snap.data_size     = BtrfsHelper.subvolume_size(workspace_dest)
   398→      end
   399→    end
   400→
   401→    snap.save!
   402→    snap
   403→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   404→    raise Error, "Failed to create snapshot: #{e.message}"
   405→  rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   406→    raise Error, "Failed to snapshot filesystem layer: #{e.message}"
   407→  end
   408→
   409→  # Legacy alias kept for backward compatibility (used by existing API endpoint).
   410→  def snapshot(sandbox:, name: nil, **opts)
   411→    snap = create_snapshot(sandbox: sandbox, name: name, layers: %w[container])
   412→    {
   413→      name: snap.name,
   414→      image: snap.docker_image,
   415→      sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   416→      created_at: snap.created_at
   417→    }
   418→  end
   419→
   420→  def list_snapshots(user:)
   421→    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   422→    Snapshot.where(user: user).order(created_at: :desc).map { |s| snapshot_json(s) }
   423→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   424→    raise Error, "Failed to list snapshots: #{e.message}"
   425→  end
   426→
   427→  def find_snapshot(user:, name:)
   428→    import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   429→    Snapshot.find_by!(user: user, name: name)
   430→  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   431→    raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   432→  end
   433→
   434→  def destroy_snapshot(user:, name:)
   435→    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: name)
   436→
   437→    if snap
   438→      # Remove Docker image
   439→      if snap.docker_image.present?
   440→        begin
   441→          Docker::Image.get(snap.docker_image).remove
   442→        rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   443→          # Already gone
   444→        rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   445→          raise Error, "Failed to remove Docker image: #{e.message}"
   446→        end
   447→      end
   448→
   449→      # Remove BTRFS snapshots
   450→      if snap.home_snapshot.present?
   451→        begin
   452→          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.home_snapshot)
   453→        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   454→          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete home snapshot #{snap.home_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   455→        end
   456→      end
   457→
   458→      if snap.data_snapshot.present?
   459→        begin
   460→          BtrfsHelper.delete_snapshot(snap.data_snapshot)
   461→        rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   462→          Rails.logger.warn("Could not delete data snapshot #{snap.data_snapshot}: #{e.message}")
   463→        end
   464→      end
   465→
   466→      snap.destroy!
   467→    else
   468→      # Legacy: try to find and remove Docker image directly
   469→      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{name}"
   470→      begin
   471→        Docker::Image.get(image_ref).remove
   472→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   473→        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{name}' not found"
   474→      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   475→        raise Error, "Failed to destroy snapshot: #{e.message}"
   476→      end
   477→    end
   478→  end
   479→
   480→  def restore(sandbox:, snapshot_name:, layers: nil)
   481→    user = sandbox.user
   482→    was_tailscale = sandbox.tailscale?
   483→
   484→    snap = Snapshot.find_by(user: user, name: snapshot_name)
   485→    requested_layers = layers&.map(&:to_s)
   486→
   487→    # Determine the Docker image to use
   488→    if snap&.docker_image.present?
   489→      image_ref = snap.docker_image
   490→    else
   491→      # Fall back to legacy naming
   492→      image_ref = "sc-snap-#{user.name}:#{snapshot_name}"
   493→    end
   494→
   495→    restore_container = requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("container")
   496→    restore_home      = snap&.home_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("home"))
   497→    restore_data      = snap&.data_snapshot.present? && (requested_layers.nil? || requested_layers.include?("data"))
   498→
   499→    # Validate the Docker image exists if we need it
   500→    if restore_container
   501→      begin
   502→        Docker::Image.get(image_ref)
   503→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   504→        raise Error, "Snapshot '#{snapshot_name}' not found"
   505→      end
   506→    end
   507→
   508→    begin
   509→      TerminalManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   510→    rescue TerminalManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   511→      # best-effort terminal cleanup
   512→    end
   513→
   514→    begin
   515→      VncManager.new.close(sandbox: sandbox)
   516→    rescue VncManager::Error, Docker::Error::DockerError
   517→      # best-effort VNC cleanup
   518→    end
   519→
   520→    if sandbox.tailscale?
   521→      TailscaleManager.new.disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   522→    end
   523→
   524→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   525→      begin
   526→        old_container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   527→        old_container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   528→        old_container.delete(force: true)
   529→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   530→        # Already gone
   531→      end
   532→    end
   533→
   534→    # ── Restore home directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   535→    if restore_home
   536→      home_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home"
   537→      begin
   538→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.home_snapshot, home_target)
   539→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   540→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore home snapshot: #{e.message}")
   541→      end
   542→    end
   543→
   544→    # ── Restore data directory ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   545→    if restore_data
   546→      if snap.data_subdir.present?
   547→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}/#{snap.data_subdir}".chomp("/")
   548→      else
   549→        data_target = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   550→      end
   551→      begin
   552→        BtrfsHelper.restore_subvolume(snap.data_snapshot, data_target)
   553→      rescue BtrfsHelper::Error => e
   554→        Rails.logger.warn("Could not restore data snapshot: #{e.message}")
   555→      end
   556→    end
   557→
   558→    # ── Recreate container ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   559→    final_image = restore_container ? image_ref : sandbox.image
   560→
   561→    container = Docker::Container.create(
   562→      "name" => sandbox.full_name,
   563→      "Image" => final_image,
   564→      "Hostname" => sandbox.full_name,
   565→      "Env" => container_env(user, sandbox),
   566→      "Labels" => { "sandcastle.sandbox" => "true" },
   567→      "HostConfig" => {
   568→        "Runtime" => container_runtime,
   569→        "NetworkMode" => NETWORK_NAME,
   570→        "Binds" => volume_binds(user, sandbox),
   571→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
   572→      },
   573→      "NetworkingConfig" => {
   574→        "EndpointsConfig" => { NETWORK_NAME => {} }
   575→      }
   576→    )
   577→
   578→    container.start
   579→    sandbox.update!(container_id: container.id, image: final_image, status: "running")
   580→
   581→    # Pre-write Traefik routes so they're active immediately after restore.
   582→    TerminalManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
   583→    VncManager.new.prepare_traefik_config(sandbox) if sandbox.vnc_enabled?
   584→
   585→    if was_tailscale && user.tailscale_enabled?
   586→      TailscaleManager.new.connect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   587→    end
   588→
   589→    sandbox
   590→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   591→    raise Error, "Failed to restore snapshot: #{e.message}"
   592→  end

   594→  # Import legacy Docker-only snapshots as DB records (idempotent).
   595→  def import_legacy_snapshots(user)
   596→    repo_prefix = "sc-snap-#{user.name}"
   597→
   598→    Docker::Image.all.each do |img|
   599→      repo_tags = img.info["RepoTags"] || []
   600→      repo_tags.each do |tag|
   601→        repo, tag_name = tag.split(":")
   602→        next unless repo == repo_prefix
   603→        next if tag_name.blank?
   604→        next if Snapshot.exists?(user: user, name: tag_name)
   605→
   606→        Snapshot.create!(
   607→          user: user,
   608→          name: tag_name,
   609→          docker_image: tag,
   610→          docker_size: img.info["Size"],
   611→          source_sandbox: img.info.dig("Comment")&.delete_prefix("sandbox:"),
   612→          created_at: Time.at(img.info["Created"] || Time.current.to_i)
   613→        )
   614→      end
   615→    end
   616→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError, ActiveRecord::RecordInvalid => e
   617→    Rails.logger.warn("Legacy snapshot import failed: #{e.message}")
   618→  end

   620→  def snapshot_json(snap)
   621→    {
   622→      name: snap.name,
   623→      label: snap.label,
   624→      source_sandbox: snap.source_sandbox,
   625→      layers: snap.layers,
   626→      docker_image: snap.docker_image,
   627→      docker_size: snap.docker_size,
   628→      home_size: snap.home_size,
   629→      data_size: snap.data_size,
   630→      total_size: snap.total_size,
   631→      created_at: snap.created_at
   632→    }
   633→  end

   635→  def connect_info(sandbox:)
   636→    user = sandbox.user.name
   637→
   638→    unless sandbox.tailscale?
   639→      raise Error, "SSH access requires Tailscale. Enable Tailscale on your account and connect this sandbox to your tailnet."
   640→    end
   641→
   642→    ts_ip = wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   643→    raise Error, "Tailscale IP not available — is the Tailscale sidecar running?" unless ts_ip.present?
   644→
   645→    {
   646→      host: ts_ip,
   647→      port: 22,
   648→      user: user,
   649→      command: "ssh #{user}@#{ts_ip}",
   650→      tailscale_ip: ts_ip
   651→    }
   652→  end
   653→
   654→  private
   655→
   656→  def wait_for_tailscale_ip(sandbox:, max_attempts: 30, delay: 0.5)
   657→    # Wait for the sandbox to be provisioned (background job) and Tailscale IP to be assigned
   658→    max_attempts.times do
   659→      sandbox.reload # Refresh from DB to get latest status
   660→
   661→      # If sandbox isn't running yet, keep waiting (provision job in progress)
   662→      if sandbox.status != "running"
   663→        sleep delay
   664→        next
   665→      end
   666→
   667→      # Sandbox is running, try to get Tailscale IP
   668→      ts_ip = TailscaleManager.new.sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox)
   669→      return ts_ip if ts_ip.present?
   670→
   671→      sleep delay
   672→    end
   673→    Rails.logger.warn("Tailscale IP not available for sandbox #{sandbox.id} after #{max_attempts} attempts (status: #{sandbox.status})")
   674→    nil
   675→  end
   676→
   677→  def connect_to_network(container)
   678→    network = Docker::Network.get(NETWORK_NAME)
   679→    network.connect(container.id)
   680→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   681→    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: network #{NETWORK_NAME} not found, skipping network connection")
   682→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   683→    Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: failed to connect container to #{NETWORK_NAME}: #{e.message}")
   684→  end

   686→  def container_env(user, sandbox)
   687→    env = [
   688→      "SANDCASTLE_USER=#{user.name}",
   689→      "SANDCASTLE_SSH_KEY=#{user.ssh_public_key}"
   690→    ]
   691→    env << "USER_EMAIL=#{user.email_address}" if user.email_address.present?
   692→    env << "USER_FULLNAME=#{user.full_name}" if user.full_name.present?
   693→    env << "GITHUB_USERNAME=#{user.github_username}" if user.github_username.present?
   694→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_ENABLED=#{sandbox.vnc_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
   695→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_GEOMETRY=#{sandbox.vnc_geometry}"
   696→    env << "SANDCASTLE_VNC_DEPTH=#{sandbox.vnc_depth}"
   697→    env << "SANDCASTLE_DOCKER_ENABLED=#{sandbox.docker_enabled? ? '1' : '0'}"
   698→    env
   699→  end
   700→
   701→  def container_runtime
   702→    @container_runtime ||= begin
   703→      runtimes = Docker.info["Runtimes"] || {}
   704→      if runtimes.key?("sysbox-runc")
   705→        "sysbox-runc"
   706→      else
   707→        Rails.logger.warn("SandboxManager: sysbox-runc not available, falling back to runc (Docker-in-Docker will not work inside sandboxes)")
   708→        "runc"
   709→      end
   710→    end
   711→  end
   712→
   713→  # mkdir_p with self-healing: if EACCES, fix the parent dir's ownership
   714→  # via a short-lived Docker container (only way when running as non-root
   715→  # inside a container with Docker socket access).
   716→  def ensure_dir(path)
   717→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(path)
   718→  rescue Errno::EACCES
   719→    docker_chown(File.dirname(path))
   720→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(path)
   721→  end
   722→
   723→  # Ensure a bind-mounted directory is world-writable so the sandbox user
   724→  # (non-root inside the Sysbox container) can write to it.
   725→  def prepare_bind_mount(path)
   726→    stat = File.stat(path)
   727→    return if stat.mode & 0o777 == 0o777
   728→    return if system("/usr/bin/sudo", "-n", "/usr/bin/chmod", "777", path)
   729→    docker_chmod(path, "777")
   730→  rescue Errno::ENOENT
   731→    # directory disappeared — race condition, ignore
   732→  end
   733→
   734→  # Fix ownership and permissions of a host path via a busybox container.
   735→  def docker_chown(path)
   736→    docker_run_fix(path, "sh", "-c", "chown #{Process.uid}:#{Process.gid} /mnt && chmod 755 /mnt")
   737→  end
   738→
   739→  def docker_chmod(path, mode)
   740→    docker_run_fix(path, "chmod", mode, "/mnt")
   741→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   742→    Rails.logger.warn("docker_chmod(#{path}) failed: #{e.message}")
   743→  end
   744→
   745→  def docker_run_fix(host_path, *cmd)
   746→    image = fix_image
   747→    c = Docker::Container.create(
   748→      "Image" => image, "Cmd" => cmd,
   749→      "HostConfig" => { "Binds" => [ "#{host_path}:/mnt" ] }
   750→    )
   751→    c.start
   752→    result = c.wait(30)
   753→    exit_code = result&.dig("StatusCode") || -1
   754→    unless exit_code == 0
   755→      raise Error, "docker_run_fix failed (exit #{exit_code}) for #{host_path}: #{cmd.join(' ')}"
   756→    end
   757→  ensure
   758→    c&.delete(force: true) rescue nil
   759→  end
   760→
   761→  # Pick an image guaranteed to be on this Docker daemon.
   762→  # Prefer busybox (tiny), fall back to alpine, then any local image.
   763→  def fix_image
   764→    %w[busybox:latest alpine:latest].each do |img|
   765→      begin
   766→        return img if Docker::Image.get(img)
   767→      rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   768→        next
   769→      end
   770→    end
   771→    # Last resort: use any image already present
   772→    all = Docker::Image.all
   773→    raise Error, "No local images available for docker_run_fix" if all.empty?
   774→    tags = all.first.info["RepoTags"]
   775→    tags&.first || all.first.id
   776→  end
   777→
   778→  def volume_binds(user, sandbox)
   779→    binds = []
   780→    if sandbox.mount_home
   781→      binds << "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/home:/home/#{user.name}"
   782→    end
   783→    if sandbox.persistent_volume && sandbox.volume_path
   784→      binds << "#{sandbox.volume_path}:/workspace"
   785→    end
   786→    if sandbox.data_path.present?
   787→      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/data/#{sandbox.data_path}".chomp("/")
   788→      binds << "#{host_path}:/data"
   789→    end
   790→    # Chrome profile persistence: mount separately if not mounting full home
   791→    if user.chrome_persist_profile? && !sandbox.mount_home
   792→      host_path = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/chrome-profile"
   793→      binds << "#{host_path}:/home/#{user.name}/.config/google-chrome"
   794→    end
   795→    binds
   796→  end
   797→end

1→class TailscaleManager
     2→  DATA_DIR = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
     3→  TAILSCALE_IMAGE = "tailscale/tailscale:latest"
     4→  TAILSCALE_TAG = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_TAILSCALE_TAG", "").presence
     5→  LOGIN_URL_PATTERN = %r{https://login\.tailscale\.com/\S+}
     6→
     7→  # Stable Tailscale machine name for this server's sidecar containers.
     8→  # Derived from SANDCASTLE_NAME (set in .env) or falls back to the system hostname.
     9→  # Slugified: lowercase, non-alphanumeric runs → "-", leading/trailing "-" stripped.
    10→  TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME = begin
    11→    name = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_NAME", "").presence || Socket.gethostname
    12→    "sc-" + name.downcase.gsub(/[^a-z0-9]+/, "-").gsub(/\A-+|-+\z/, "")
    13→  end
    14→
    15→  class Error < StandardError; end
    16→
    17→  # Restore sidecar from persisted tailscaled.state without re-authentication.
    18→  # Used after reinstall or crash when interactive-login state survives on disk.
    19→  # Note: we intentionally skip checking File.exist? for the state file because
    20→  # the tailscale directory is root-owned (drwx------) and unreadable by the
    21→  # sandcastle process. Containerboot will handle missing state gracefully.
    22→  def restore_from_state(user:)
    23→    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?
    24→
    25→    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
    26→    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
    27→    subnet = subnet_for(user)
    28→
    29→    pull_image
    30→    create_network(network_name, subnet)
    31→    remove_existing_container(container_name)
    32→    # Intentionally skip clear_tailscale_state — we rely on existing saved credentials
    33→    # Use raw tailscaled (same as interactive flow) — NOT containerboot.
    34→    # Containerboot runs `tailscale logout` on SIGTERM, which would expire the
    35→    # saved node key. Raw tailscaled exits cleanly without touching auth state.
    36→    container = create_sidecar(
    37→      name: container_name,
    38→      user: user,
    39→      network: network_name,
    40→      subnet: subnet,
    41→      auth_key: nil
    42→    )
    43→    container.start
    44→
    45→    # Re-advertise routes after tailscaled reconnects — the subnet may have changed
    46→    # after a reinstall (Docker reallocates from the pool). No --reset so we preserve
    47→    # credentials and other settings in the persisted state. Also re-applies the
    48→    # canonical hostname so it reflects the current SANDCASTLE_NAME.
    49→    container.exec([
    50→      "sh", "-c",
    51→      "sleep 3 && tailscale up" \
    52→      " --advertise-routes=#{subnet}" \
    53→      " --accept-routes" \
    54→      " --hostname=#{TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME}" \
    55→      " --timeout=60s &"
    56→    ])
    57→
    58→    user.update!(
    59→      tailscale_state: "enabled",
    60→      tailscale_auto_connect: true,
    61→      tailscale_container_id: container.id,
    62→      tailscale_network: network_name,
    63→      tailscale_subnet: subnet
    64→    )
    65→    user
    66→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    67→    cleanup_on_failure(user)
    68→    raise Error, "Failed to restore Tailscale from state: #{e.message}"
    69→  end

    71→  # Legacy: one-shot enable with an auth key (still supported for automation)
    72→  def enable(user:, auth_key:)
    73→    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?
    74→
    75→    network_name, container = create_and_start_sidecar(user: user, auth_key: auth_key)
    76→
    77→    user.update!(
    78→      tailscale_state: "enabled",
    79→      tailscale_auto_connect: true,
    80→      tailscale_container_id: container.id,
    81→      tailscale_network: network_name
    82→    )
    83→    save_auth_key(user, auth_key)
    84→    user
    85→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
    86→    cleanup_on_failure(user)
    87→    raise Error, "Failed to enable Tailscale: #{e.message}"
    88→  end

    90→  # Phase 1: create and start sidecar, return immediately.
    91→  # Called from TailscaleLoginJob — user is already in "pending" state.
    92→  def start_login(user:)
    93→    raise Error, "Tailscale already active" if user.tailscale_enabled?
    94→
    95→    # If there's an old container, clean up first
    96→    cleanup_sidecar(user) if user.tailscale_container_id.present?
    97→
    98→    hostname = Rails.cache.read("ts_hostname:#{user.id}")
    99→    network_name, container = create_and_start_sidecar(user: user, auth_key: nil, hostname: hostname)
   100→
   101→    user.update!(
   102→      tailscale_container_id: container.id,
   103→      tailscale_network: network_name
   104→    )
   105→
   106→    { status: "starting" }
   107→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   108→    cleanup_on_failure(user)
   109→    raise Error, "Failed to start Tailscale login: #{e.message}"
   110→  end
   111→
   112→  # Phase 2: progressive login status check
   113→  # Returns: { status: "starting" | "waiting_for_url" | "login_ready" | "authenticated" | "error" }
   114→  def check_login(user:)
   115→    raise Error, "No pending login" unless user.tailscale_pending?
   116→    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found" if user.tailscale_container_id.blank?
   117→
   118→    container = Docker::Container.get(user.tailscale_container_id)
   119→    running = container.json.dig("State", "Running")
   120→    return { status: "starting", message: "Starting sidecar container..." } unless running
   121→
   122→    # Kick off `tailscale up` in the background if not already done
   123→    cache_key = "ts_login_started:#{user.id}"
   124→    unless Rails.cache.read(cache_key)
   125→      subnet = subnet_for(user)
   126→      tag = Rails.cache.read("ts_tag:#{user.id}") || TAILSCALE_TAG
   127→      tag_flag = tag ? " --advertise-tags=#{tag}" : ""
   128→      container.exec([
   129→        "sh", "-c",
   130→        "tailscale up --reset" \
   131→        " --advertise-routes=#{subnet}" \
   132→        "#{tag_flag}" \
   133→        " --hostname=#{container.json.dig("Config", "Hostname")}" \
   134→        " --timeout=120s &"
   135→      ])
   136→      Rails.cache.write(cache_key, true, expires_in: 5.minutes)
   137→      return { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
   138→    end
   139→
   140→    # Check tailscale status for auth progress
   141→    status_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "status", "--json" ])
   142→    if status_out.first.any?
   143→      ts_status = JSON.parse(status_out.first.join)
   144→      case ts_status["BackendState"]
   145→      when "Running"
   146→        Rails.cache.delete(cache_key)
   147→        user.update!(tailscale_state: "enabled", tailscale_auto_connect: true)
   148→        ip_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "ip", "--4" ])
   149→        tailscale_ip = ip_out.first.first&.strip if ip_out.first.any?
   150→        return {
   151→          status: "authenticated",
   152→          tailscale_ip: tailscale_ip,
   153→          hostname: ts_status.dig("Self", "HostName"),
   154→          tailnet: ts_status.dig("MagicDNSSuffix")
   155→        }
   156→      when "NeedsLogin"
   157→        auth_url = ts_status["AuthURL"]
   158→        if auth_url.present?
   159→          return { status: "login_ready", login_url: auth_url, message: "Click the link to authenticate" }
   160→        end
   161→      end
   162→    end
   163→
   164→    { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
   165→  rescue JSON::ParserError
   166→    { status: "waiting_for_url", message: "Waiting for login URL..." }
   167→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   168→    Rails.cache.delete("ts_login_started:#{user.id}")
   169→    user.update!(tailscale_state: "disabled", tailscale_container_id: nil, tailscale_network: nil)
   170→    raise Error, "Sidecar container disappeared"
   171→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   172→    raise Error, "Failed to check login: #{e.message}"
   173→  end
   174→
   175→  def disable(user:)
   176→    raise Error, "Tailscale not active" if user.tailscale_disabled?
   177→
   178→    Rails.cache.delete("ts_login_started:#{user.id}")
   179→
   180→    # Disconnect all sandboxes first
   181→    user.sandboxes.active.where(tailscale: true).find_each do |sandbox|
   182→      disconnect_sandbox(sandbox: sandbox)
   183→    end
   184→
   185→    cleanup_sidecar(user)
   186→
   187→    user.update!(
   188→      tailscale_state: "disabled",
   189→      tailscale_container_id: nil,
   190→      tailscale_network: nil
   191→    )
   192→    delete_auth_key(user)
   193→    user
   194→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   195→    raise Error, "Failed to disable Tailscale: #{e.message}"
   196→  end
   197→
   198→  def status(user:)
   199→    raise Error, "Tailscale not active" unless user.tailscale_enabled? || user.tailscale_pending?
   200→    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found" if user.tailscale_container_id.blank?
   201→
   202→    container = Docker::Container.get(user.tailscale_container_id)
   203→    running = container.json.dig("State", "Running")
   204→
   205→    ts_sandboxes = user.sandboxes.active.where(tailscale: true)
   206→    sandbox_ips = ts_sandboxes.map do |sb|
   207→      ip = sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sb)
   208→      { name: sb.name, ip: ip }
   209→    end
   210→
   211→    result = {
   212→      running: running,
   213→      container_id: user.tailscale_container_id[0..11],
   214→      network: user.tailscale_network,
   215→      connected_sandboxes: ts_sandboxes.count,
   216→      sandboxes: sandbox_ips
   217→    }
   218→
   219→    if running
   220→      ip_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "ip", "--4" ])
   221→      result[:tailscale_ip] = ip_out.first.first&.strip if ip_out.first.any?
   222→
   223→      status_out = container.exec([ "tailscale", "status", "--json" ])
   224→      if status_out.first.any?
   225→        begin
   226→          ts_status = JSON.parse(status_out.first.join)
   227→          result[:hostname] = ts_status.dig("Self", "HostName")
   228→          result[:tailnet] = ts_status.dig("MagicDNSSuffix")
   229→          result[:online] = ts_status.dig("Self", "Online")
   230→          tags = ts_status.dig("Self", "Tags")
   231→          result[:tags] = tags if tags.present?
   232→        rescue JSON::ParserError
   233→          # Status not available yet
   234→        end
   235→      end
   236→    else
   237→      # Grab last 20 lines of container logs for debugging
   238→      logs = container.logs(stdout: true, stderr: true, tail: 20)
   239→      result[:logs] = logs.encode("UTF-8", invalid: :replace, undef: :replace).strip if logs.present?
   240→      result[:exit_code] = container.json.dig("State", "ExitCode")
   241→      result[:error_reason] = container.json.dig("State", "Error")
   242→    end
   243→
   244→    result
   245→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   246→    raise Error, "Sidecar container not found — try disabling and re-enabling Tailscale"
   247→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   248→    raise Error, "Failed to get Tailscale status: #{e.message}"
   249→  end
   250→
   251→  def connect_sandbox(sandbox:)
   252→    user = sandbox.user
   253→    raise Error, "Tailscale not enabled for user" unless user.tailscale_enabled?
   254→    raise Error, "Sandbox not running" unless sandbox.container_id.present?
   255→
   256→    network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)
   257→    network.connect(sandbox.container_id)
   258→    sandbox.update!(tailscale: true)
   259→    sandbox
   260→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   261→    raise Error, "Failed to connect sandbox to Tailscale: #{e.message}"
   262→  end
   263→
   264→  def disconnect_sandbox(sandbox:)
   265→    user = sandbox.user
   266→    return sandbox unless sandbox.tailscale? && user.tailscale_network.present?
   267→
   268→    if sandbox.container_id.present?
   269→      begin
   270→        network = Docker::Network.get(user.tailscale_network)
   271→        network.disconnect(sandbox.container_id)
   272→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError, Docker::Error::DockerError
   273→        # Network/container already gone or container not connected — desired state reached
   274→      end
   275→    end
   276→
   277→    sandbox.update!(tailscale: false)
   278→    sandbox
   279→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError => e
   280→    raise Error, "Failed to disconnect sandbox from Tailscale: #{e.message}"
   281→  end
   282→
   283→  def sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox:)
   284→    user = sandbox.user
   285→    return nil unless sandbox.tailscale? && sandbox.container_id.present? && user.tailscale_network.present?
   286→
   287→    container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
   288→    container.json.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks", user.tailscale_network, "IPAddress")
   289→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   290→    nil
   291→  end
   292→
   293→  def auth_key_path(user)
   294→    File.join(DATA_DIR, "users", user.name, "tailscale", ".auth_key")
   295→  end
   296→
   297→  private
   298→
   299→  def save_auth_key(user, auth_key)
   300→    path = auth_key_path(user)
   301→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(File.dirname(path))
   302→    File.write(path, auth_key)
   303→    File.chmod(0o600, path)
   304→  end
   305→
   306→  def delete_auth_key(user)
   307→    path = auth_key_path(user)
   308→    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
   309→  rescue Errno::ENOENT
   310→    # already gone
   311→  end
   312→
   313→  def create_and_start_sidecar(user:, auth_key:, hostname: nil)
   314→    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
   315→    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
   316→    subnet = subnet_for(user)
   317→
   318→    pull_image
   319→    create_network(network_name, subnet)
   320→    remove_existing_container(container_name)
   321→    clear_tailscale_state(user)
   322→    container = create_sidecar(
   323→      name: container_name,
   324→      user: user,
   325→      network: network_name,
   326→      subnet: subnet,
   327→      auth_key: auth_key,
   328→      hostname: hostname
   329→    )
   330→    container.start
   331→
   332→    # Persist the subnet so it survives reinstalls (subnet_for reads this first).
   333→    user.update_column(:tailscale_subnet, subnet)
   334→
   335→    [ network_name, container ]
   336→  end
   337→
   338→  def remove_existing_container(name)
   339→    container = Docker::Container.get(name)
   340→    container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   341→    container.delete(force: true)
   342→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   343→    # No existing container
   344→  end
   345→
   346→  # Remove stale tailscaled.state so the daemon starts fresh.
   347→  # Stale state causes tailscaled to immediately set up VPN routes before
   348→  # authentication, which silently drops all normal internet traffic.
   349→  def clear_tailscale_state(user)
   350→    state_file = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/tailscale/tailscaled.state"
   351→    File.delete(state_file) if File.exist?(state_file)
   352→  rescue Errno::ENOENT
   353→    # already gone
   354→  end
   355→
   356→  def cleanup_sidecar(user)
   357→    # Try by stored container ID first, then by name as fallback
   358→    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
   359→    [user.tailscale_container_id, container_name].compact.uniq.each do |ref|
   360→      begin
   361→        container = Docker::Container.get(ref)
   362→        container.stop(t: 5) rescue nil
   363→        container.delete(force: true)
   364→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   365→        # Already gone
   366→      end
   367→    end
   368→
   369→    # Clean up network by stored name, then by convention
   370→    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
   371→    [user.tailscale_network, network_name].compact.uniq.each do |ref|
   372→      begin
   373→        Docker::Network.get(ref).delete
   374→      rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   375→        # Already gone
   376→      end
   377→    end
   378→  end
   379→
   380→  def cleanup_on_failure(user)
   381→    container_name = "sc-ts-#{user.name}"
   382→    network_name = "sc-ts-net-#{user.name}"
   383→    begin
   384→      Docker::Container.get(container_name).delete(force: true)
   385→    rescue StandardError
   386→      nil
   387→    end
   388→    begin
   389→      Docker::Network.get(network_name).delete
   390→    rescue StandardError
   391→      nil
   392→    end
   393→    user.update!(tailscale_state: "disabled", tailscale_container_id: nil, tailscale_network: nil)
   394→  end
   395→
   396→  def pull_image
   397→    Docker::Image.get(TAILSCALE_IMAGE)
   398→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   399→    Docker::Image.create("fromImage" => TAILSCALE_IMAGE)
   400→  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
   401→    raise Error, "Failed to pull #{TAILSCALE_IMAGE} — check network connectivity"
   402→  end
   403→
   404→  def subnet_for(user)
   405→    # 1. Use the subnet stored in the DB — stable across Docker/reinstalls
   406→    return user.tailscale_subnet if user.tailscale_subnet.present?
   407→
   408→    # 2. If the network already exists on Docker, read its actual subnet
   409→    begin
   410→      network = Docker::Network.get("sc-ts-net-#{user.name}")
   411→      ipam = network.info.dig("IPAM", "Config")
   412→      return ipam.first["Subnet"] if ipam&.first
   413→    rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   414→      # Network doesn't exist yet — fall through to generate a random /24
   415→    end
   416→
   417→    # 3. Generate a random /24 from the pool (first allocation)
   418→    base = ENV["DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE"]
   419→    if base
   420→      parts = base.split("/").first.split(".").map(&:to_i)
   421→    else
   422→      parts = [ 10, rand(1..254), 0, 0 ]
   423→    end
   424→    parts[2] = rand(1..254)
   425→    "#{parts[0]}.#{parts[1]}.#{parts[2]}.0/24"
   426→  end
   427→
   428→  def create_network(name, subnet)
   429→    Docker::Network.get(name)
   430→  rescue Docker::Error::NotFoundError
   431→    Docker::Network.create(
   432→      name,
   433→      "Driver" => "bridge",
   434→      "IPAM" => {
   435→        "Config" => [ { "Subnet" => subnet } ]
   436→      }
   437→    )
   438→  end
   439→
   440→  def create_sidecar(name:, user:, network:, subnet:, auth_key:, hostname: nil)
   441→    state_dir = "#{DATA_DIR}/users/#{user.name}/tailscale"
   442→    ts_hostname = hostname.presence || TAILSCALE_HOSTNAME
   443→
   444→    config = {
   445→      "Image" => TAILSCALE_IMAGE,
   446→      "name" => name,
   447→      "Hostname" => ts_hostname,
   448→      "HostConfig" => {
   449→        "NetworkMode" => network,
   450→        "CapAdd" => [ "NET_ADMIN", "SYS_MODULE" ],
   451→        "Devices" => [
   452→          { "PathOnHost" => "/dev/net/tun", "PathInContainer" => "/dev/net/tun", "CgroupPermissions" => "rwm" }
   453→        ],
   454→        "Sysctls" => { "net.ipv4.ip_forward" => "1" },
   455→        "Binds" => [ "#{state_dir}:/var/lib/tailscale" ],
   456→        "RestartPolicy" => { "Name" => "unless-stopped" }
   457→      }
   458→    }
   459→
   460→    if auth_key.present?
   461→      # Use containerboot (default entrypoint) with auth key for automated flow
   462→      config["Env"] = [
   463→        "TS_STATE_DIR=/var/lib/tailscale",
   464→        "TS_HOSTNAME=#{ts_hostname}",
   465→        "TS_EXTRA_ARGS=--advertise-routes=#{subnet} --accept-routes#{TAILSCALE_TAG ? " --advertise-tags=#{TAILSCALE_TAG}" : ""}",
   466→        "TS_AUTH_ONCE=true",
   467→        "TS_AUTHKEY=#{auth_key}"
   468→      ]
   469→    else
   470→      # Run tailscaled directly — we manage login via `tailscale up`.
   471→      # Also used for restore_from_state: raw tailscaled exits without running
   472→      # `tailscale logout` on SIGTERM, preserving saved credentials in state file.
   473→      config["Entrypoint"] = [ "tailscaled", "--state=/var/lib/tailscale/tailscaled.state" ]
   474→      config["Cmd"] = []
   475→    end
   476→
   477→    Docker::Container.create(config)
   478→  end
   479→end
   480→

1→class TerminalManager
     2→  DATA_DIR     = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_DATA_DIR", "/data")
     3→  NETWORK_NAME = "sandcastle-web"
     4→  DYNAMIC_DIR  = File.join(DATA_DIR, "traefik", "dynamic")
     5→  TMUX_PORT  = 7681
     6→  SHELL_PORT = 7682
     7→
     8→  class Error < StandardError; end
     9→
    10→  # Opens a web terminal for the given sandbox.
    11→  # type: "tmux" (default) or "shell"
    12→  # Writes Traefik config (idempotent) and returns the terminal URL.
    13→  def open(sandbox:, type: "tmux")
    14→    raise Error, "Sandbox is not running" unless sandbox.status == "running"
    15→
    16→    write_traefik_config(sandbox)
    17→    terminal_url(sandbox, type)
    18→  end
    19→
    20→  # Pre-writes the Traefik config without requiring the sandbox to be open.
    21→  # Called at container start so routes are ready immediately.
    22→  def prepare_traefik_config(sandbox)
    23→    write_traefik_config(sandbox)
    24→  end
    25→
    26→  # Deletes the Traefik config for the given sandbox.
    27→  def close(sandbox:)
    28→    delete_traefik_config(sandbox)
    29→  end
    30→
    31→  # Scans terminal-*.yml files; removes configs for sandboxes that no longer exist or aren't running.
    32→  def cleanup_orphaned
    33→    Dir.glob(File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "terminal-*.yml")).each do |path|
    34→      match = File.basename(path).match(/\Aterminal-(\d+)\.yml\z/)
    35→      next unless match
    36→
    37→      sandbox_id = match[1].to_i
    38→      sandbox = Sandbox.find_by(id: sandbox_id)
    39→
    40→      if sandbox.nil? || sandbox.status != "running"
    41→        File.delete(path)
    42→        Rails.logger.info("TerminalManager: removed orphaned Traefik config #{File.basename(path)}")
    43→      end
    44→    end
    45→  end
    46→
    47→  private
    48→
    49→  def terminal_url(sandbox, type)
    50→    "/terminal/#{sandbox.id}/#{type}"
    51→  end
    52→
    53→  def write_traefik_config(sandbox)
    54→    FileUtils.mkdir_p(DYNAMIC_DIR)
    55→    config_path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "terminal-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
    56→    return false if File.exist?(config_path)
    57→
    58→    host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    59→    id   = sandbox.id
    60→    container = sandbox.full_name
    61→
    62→    if ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"] == "selfsigned"
    63→      tmux_rule  = "HostRegexp(`.+`) && PathPrefix(`/terminal/#{id}/tmux`)"
    64→      shell_rule = "HostRegexp(`.+`) && PathPrefix(`/terminal/#{id}/shell`)"
    65→    else
    66→      tmux_rule  = "Host(`#{host}`) && PathPrefix(`/terminal/#{id}/tmux`)"
    67→      shell_rule = "Host(`#{host}`) && PathPrefix(`/terminal/#{id}/shell`)"
    68→    end
    69→
    70→    config = {
    71→      "http" => {
    72→        "routers" => {
    73→          "terminal-#{id}-tmux" => {
    74→            "rule"        => tmux_rule,
    75→            "service"     => "terminal-#{id}-tmux",
    76→            "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
    77→            "tls"         => tls_config,
    78→            "middlewares" => [ "terminal-auth-#{id}", "terminal-#{id}-strip-tmux" ],
    79→            "priority"    => 100
    80→          },
    81→          "terminal-#{id}-shell" => {
    82→            "rule"        => shell_rule,
    83→            "service"     => "terminal-#{id}-shell",
    84→            "entryPoints" => [ "websecure" ],
    85→            "tls"         => tls_config,
    86→            "middlewares" => [ "terminal-auth-#{id}", "terminal-#{id}-strip-shell" ],
    87→            "priority"    => 100
    88→          }
    89→        },
    90→        "middlewares" => {
    91→          "terminal-auth-#{id}" => {
    92→            "forwardAuth" => {
    93→              "address"            => "http://sandcastle-web:80/terminal/auth",
    94→              "trustForwardHeader" => true
    95→            }
    96→          },
    97→          "terminal-#{id}-strip-tmux" => {
    98→            "stripPrefix" => { "prefixes" => [ "/terminal/#{id}/tmux" ] }
    99→          },
   100→          "terminal-#{id}-strip-shell" => {
   101→            "stripPrefix" => { "prefixes" => [ "/terminal/#{id}/shell" ] }
   102→          }
   103→        },
   104→        "services" => {
   105→          "terminal-#{id}-tmux" => {
   106→            "loadBalancer" => {
   107→              "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://#{container}:#{TMUX_PORT}" } ]
   108→            }
   109→          },
   110→          "terminal-#{id}-shell" => {
   111→            "loadBalancer" => {
   112→              "servers" => [ { "url" => "http://#{container}:#{SHELL_PORT}" } ]
   113→            }
   114→          }
   115→        }
   116→      }
   117→    }
   118→
   119→    File.write(config_path, config.to_yaml)
   120→    true
   121→  end
   122→
   123→  def tls_config
   124→    case ENV["SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE"]
   125→    when "selfsigned", "mkcert"
   126→      {}
   127→    else
   128→      { "certResolver" => "letsencrypt" }
   129→    end
   130→  end
   131→
   132→  def delete_traefik_config(sandbox)
   133→    path = File.join(DYNAMIC_DIR, "terminal-#{sandbox.id}.yml")
   134→    File.delete(path) if File.exist?(path)
   135→  end
   136→end
   137→

1→services:
     2→  traefik:
     3→    image: traefik:v3.6.8
     4→    container_name: sandcastle-traefik
     5→    restart: unless-stopped
     6→    ports:
     7→      - "80:80"
     8→      - "443:443"
     9→      - "${SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN:-3000}-${SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX:-3099}:${SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN:-3000}-${SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX:-3099}"
    10→    volumes:
    11→      - /data/traefik/traefik.yml:/etc/traefik/traefik.yml
    12→      - /data/traefik/dynamic:/data/dynamic:ro
    13→      - /data/traefik/acme.json:/data/acme.json
    14→      - /data/traefik/certs:/data/certs:ro
    15→    networks:
    16→      - sandcastle-web
    17→
    18→  postgres:
    19→    image: postgres:18
    20→    restart: unless-stopped
    21→    volumes:
    22→      - /data/postgres:/var/lib/postgresql
    23→      - ./docker/postgres/init-databases.sh:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/init-databases.sh:ro
    24→    environment:
    25→      POSTGRES_USER: sandcastle
    26→      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD:-sandcastle}
    27→      POSTGRES_DB: sandcastle_production
    28→    healthcheck:
    29→      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U sandcastle -d sandcastle_production"]
    30→      interval: 5s
    31→      timeout: 5s
    32→      retries: 5
    33→    networks:
    34→      - sandcastle-web
    35→
    36→  web:
    37→    image: ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle:latest
    38→    container_name: sandcastle-web
    39→    group_add:
    40→      - "${DOCKER_GID:-988}"

1→# Sandcastle Networking
     2→
     3→Sandcastle runs an **isolated Docker daemon** ("dockyard") separate from the host's system Docker. All container networking — sandboxes, terminals, VNC, Tailscale — runs through this daemon and is confined to RFC 1918 private IP ranges that you control.
     4→
     5→## Configuration variables
     6→
     7→Set these in `sandcastle.env` before running `installer.sh install`.
     8→
     9→### `SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET` (default: `10.89.0.0/16`)
    10→
    11→The **single knob** for all Sandcastle networking. Every Docker network is carved from this /16. Change it if the default conflicts with your LAN or existing Docker setup.
    12→
    13→Must be an RFC 1918 private range:
    14→
    15→| Range | Example value |
    16→|---|---|
    17→| Class A | `10.89.0.0/16` (default) |
    18→| Class B | `172.20.0.0/16` |
    19→| Class C | `192.168.89.0/16`* |
    20→
    21→> \* 192.168.x.x is a /16 in the RFC but many networks are already in use there — prefer 10.x.x.x or 172.16–31.x.x.
    22→
    23→The installer automatically derives `DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR`, `DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR`, and `DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE` from this value. You only need the individual overrides below if you need fine-grained control.
    24→
    25→---
    26→
    27→### Derived variables (override only if needed)
    28→
    29→#### `DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR` (derived: `<net>.0.1/24`)
    30→
    31→The **gateway IP and mask** for dockyard's default bridge interface (`sc_docker0` on the host). This is what Docker calls `bip`. Every container on the default bridge reaches the host via this IP.
    32→
    33→Example for `10.89.0.0/16`: `10.89.0.1/24`
    34→
    35→#### `DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR` (derived: `<net>.0.0/24`)
    36→
    37→The **container address range** on the default bridge. Limits IPs Docker assigns to containers here, keeping them predictable and distinct from subnets allocated to user-defined networks.
    38→
    39→Example for `10.89.0.0/16`: `10.89.0.0/24`
    40→
    41→#### `DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE` (derived: same as `SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET`)
    42→
    43→The **address pool** from which dockyard allocates subnets for every user-defined Docker network (`sandcastle-web`, `sc-ts-net-*`, etc.). When a network is created without an explicit subnet, Docker carves a free `/24` from this range.
    44→
    45→#### `DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE` (default: `24`)
    46→
    47→The prefix length of each subnet allocated from the pool. `/24` gives 254 usable container IPs per network and allows up to 256 networks within a `/16` pool.
    48→
    49→---
    50→
    51→## Runtime networks
    52→
    53→These Docker networks are created automatically — no configuration needed.
    54→
    55→### `sandcastle-web`
    56→
    57→Created once by the installer. Subnet auto-allocated from `DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE` (first available /24).
    58→
    59→The internal "service bus" — anything that needs to talk to anything else goes through here:
    60→
    61→| Container | Purpose |
    62→|---|---|
    63→| `sandcastle-web` | Rails app |
    64→| `sc-wetty-{user}-{sandbox}` | Web terminal sidecars (SSH into sandbox) |
    65→| `sc-novnc-{sandbox}` | noVNC browser UI containers |
    66→| sandbox containers | Joined on demand when a terminal or VNC session opens |
    67→
    68→Traefik routes external HTTPS traffic into this network.
    69→
    70→### `sc-ts-net-{username}`
    71→
    72→Created by `TailscaleManager` when a user enables Tailscale. One network per user, each with an **explicitly assigned /24** derived from `DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE`:
    73→
    74→```
    75→base = DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE   # e.g. 10.89.0.0/16
    76→subnet = oct1.oct2.(1 + user_id % 255).0/24
    77→
    78→# user_id=1  → 10.89.2.0/24
    79→# user_id=2  → 10.89.3.0/24
    80→```
    81→
    82→| Container | Purpose |
    83→|---|---|
    84→| `sc-ts-{username}` | Tailscale sidecar — joins user's tailnet, advertises the /24 as a subnet route |
    85→| sandbox containers | Joined when `tailscale_auto_connect` is enabled |
    86→
    87→Because these subnets sit within `DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE`, dockyard's iptables NAT rules cover them automatically — sandbox containers get outbound internet.
    88→
    89→---
    90→
    91→## Network layout (default)
    92→
    93→```mermaid
    94→graph TB
    95→    internet(["Internet
    96→HTTPS"])
    97→    tailnet(["User Tailnet"])
    98→
    99→    subgraph host["Host Server"]
   100→        traefik["Traefik
   101→Reverse Proxy"]
   102→
   103→        subgraph private["SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET — 10.89.x.x"]
   104→
   105→            subgraph bridge_net["sc_docker0 — default bridge — 10.89.0.x"]
   106→                def_containers["Sandbox containers
   107→default network"]
   108→            end
   109→
   110→            subgraph web_net["sandcastle-web — pool-allocated, e.g. 10.89.1.x"]
   111→                rails["sandcastle-web
   112→Rails app"]
   113→                wetty["sc-wetty-user-sandbox
   114→WeTTY terminal sidecar"]
   115→                novnc["sc-novnc-sandbox
   116→noVNC browser sidecar"]
   117→                sb_web["Sandbox container
   118→joined on demand"]
   119→            end
   120→
   121→            subgraph ts_net["sc-ts-net-user — per-user, e.g. 10.89.2.x"]
   122→                ts_sidecar["sc-ts-user
   123→Tailscale sidecar
   124→advertises subnet route"]
   125→                sb_ts["Sandbox container
   126→Tailscale enabled"]
   127→            end
   128→
   129→        end
   130→    end
   131→
   132→    internet -->|"HTTPS"| traefik
   133→    traefik -->|"route"| rails
   134→    traefik -->|"route"| wetty
   135→    traefik -->|"route"| novnc
   136→    tailnet -->|"subnet route"| ts_sidecar
   137→```
   138→
   139→---
   140→
   141→## How to configure during install
   142→
   143→### Quickstart (change the private range)
   144→
   145→```bash
   146→# 1. Generate config
   147→sudo installer.sh gen-env
   148→
   149→# 2. Change the private network
   150→sed -i 's|SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET=.*|SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET=172.20.0.0/16|' sandcastle.env
   151→
   152→# 3. Install
   153→sudo installer.sh install
   154→```
   155→
   156→Or edit `sandcastle.env` manually — the relevant section looks like:
   157→
   158→```bash
   159→# ─── Dockyard (Docker + Sysbox) ──────────────────────────────────────────────
   160→
   161→DOCKYARD_ROOT=/sandcastle
   162→DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX=sc_
   163→
   164→# Private /16 from which all Sandcastle Docker networks are carved.
   165→# Must be RFC 1918: 10.x.x.x, 172.16-31.x.x, or 192.168.x.x.
   166→SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET=10.89.0.0/16
   167→#DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR=10.89.0.1/24   # uncomment to override
   168→#DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR=10.89.0.0/24    # uncomment to override
   169→#DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE=10.89.0.0/16     # uncomment to override
   170→DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE=24
   171→```
   172→
   173→### Fine-grained override example
   174→
   175→If you want the bridge on one range and the pool on another (e.g. bridge on `10.42.x.x`, pool on `10.89.x.x`):
   176→
   177→```bash
   178→SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET=10.89.0.0/16    # pool stays here
   179→DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR=10.42.0.1/24     # override bridge to a different range
   180→DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR=10.42.0.0/24
   181→```
   182→
   183→### Changing the network on an existing install
   184→
   185→> **Warning:** changing these values after install requires destroying and recreating dockyard (the isolated Docker daemon) because Docker bakes the address pool into its state. This will also recreate the `sandcastle-web` network, briefly interrupting running sandboxes.
   186→
   187→```bash
   188→# Update sandcastle.env with the new SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET value, then:
   189→sudo installer.sh reset
   190→```
   191→

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# Sandcastle installer
     3→# Usage: installer.sh [gen-env|install|reset|uninstall|help]
     4→set -euo pipefail
     5→
     6→# ═══ Colors & helpers ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     7→
     8→RED='\033[0;31m'
     9→GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    10→YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    11→BLUE='\033[0;34m'
    12→NC='\033[0m'
    13→
    14→info()  { echo -e "${BLUE}[INFO]${NC} $*"; }
    15→ok()    { echo -e "${GREEN}[OK]${NC} $*"; }
    16→warn()  { echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARN]${NC} $*"; }
    17→error() { echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $*" >&2; }
    18→die()   { error "$@"; exit 1; }
    19→
    20→WRITTEN_FILES=()
    21→wrote() { WRITTEN_FILES+=("$1"); }
    22→print_written_files() {
    23→  if [ ${#WRITTEN_FILES[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    24→    echo ""
    25→    info "Files created/updated:"
    26→    for f in "${WRITTEN_FILES[@]}"; do
    27→      echo -e "    ${f}"
    28→    done
    29→  fi
    30→  WRITTEN_FILES=()
    31→}
    32→
    33→require_root() {
    34→  [ "$(id -u)" -eq 0 ] || die "This command must be run as root (use sudo)"
    35→}
    36→
    37→# ═══ Parse command ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    38→
    39→COMMAND="${1:-install}"
    40→shift 2>/dev/null || true
    41→
    42→case "$COMMAND" in
    43→  gen-env|install|update|reset|uninstall) ;;
    44→  help|-h|--help) COMMAND="help" ;;
    45→  *) die "Unknown command: $COMMAND (use 'help' for usage)" ;;
    46→esac
    47→
    48→# ═══ Help ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    49→
    50→if [ "$COMMAND" = "help" ]; then
    51→  cat <<'USAGE'
    52→Usage: installer.sh [COMMAND] [OPTIONS]
    53→
    54→Sandcastle installer — sets up Docker (via Dockyard), Traefik, and the
    55→Sandcastle platform.
    56→
    57→Commands:
    58→  gen-env      Generate sandcastle.env config file (default: ./sandcastle.env)
    59→  install      Install or upgrade Sandcastle (default)
    60→  update       Pull latest images and restart services
    61→  reset        Tear down existing install, then reinstall
    62→  uninstall    Remove Sandcastle completely
    63→  help         Show this help message
    64→
    65→install options:
    66→  --from-backup <file>   Restore data from a backup file after installing.
    67→                         Secrets, database, and user data are loaded from the
    68→                         backup instead of being generated fresh.
    69→                         SANDCASTLE_HOST is still required in sandcastle.env.
    70→                         SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_EMAIL/PASSWORD are not required.
    71→
    72→Environment:
    73→  SANDCASTLE_ENV   Path to config file (default search order:
    74→                   ./sandcastle.env → <script_dir>/sandcastle.env →
    75→                   $SANDCASTLE_HOME/etc/sandcastle.env)
    76→
    77→Workflow (fresh install):
    78→  1. installer.sh gen-env              # generate config
    79→  2. vi sandcastle.env                 # edit to taste
    80→  3. sudo installer.sh install         # install (finds ./sandcastle.env)
    81→
    82→Workflow (restore from backup):
    83→  1. installer.sh gen-env              # generate config (set SANDCASTLE_HOST)
    84→  2. vi sandcastle.env                 # set host, TLS mode — no admin password needed
    85→  3. sudo installer.sh install --from-backup ./sandcastle-backup-*.tar.zst
    86→USAGE
    87→  exit 0
    88→fi
    89→
    90→# ═══ load_env ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    91→
    92→load_env() {
    93→  local script_dir
    94→  script_dir="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
    95→  local home="${SANDCASTLE_HOME:-/sandcastle}"
    96→
    97→  LOADED_ENV_FILE=""
    98→  if [ -n "${SANDCASTLE_ENV:-}" ]; then
    99→    [ ! -f "$SANDCASTLE_ENV" ] && die "Env file not found: $SANDCASTLE_ENV"
   100→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$(cd "$(dirname "$SANDCASTLE_ENV")" && pwd)/$(basename "$SANDCASTLE_ENV")"
   101→  elif [ -f "./sandcastle.env" ]; then
   102→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$(pwd)/sandcastle.env"
   103→  elif [ -f "$script_dir/sandcastle.env" ]; then
   104→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$script_dir/sandcastle.env"
   105→  elif [ -f "$home/etc/sandcastle.env" ]; then
   106→    LOADED_ENV_FILE="$home/etc/sandcastle.env"
   107→  fi
   108→
   109→  if [ -n "$LOADED_ENV_FILE" ]; then
   110→    set -a
   111→    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
   112→    source "$LOADED_ENV_FILE"
   113→    set +a
   114→    info "Loaded $LOADED_ENV_FILE"
   115→  fi
   116→}
   117→
   118→# ═══ derive_vars ═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   119→
   120→derive_vars() {
   121→  SANDCASTLE_HOME="${SANDCASTLE_HOME:-/sandcastle}"
   122→  APP_IMAGE="${APP_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle:latest}"
   123→  SANDBOX_IMAGE="${SANDBOX_IMAGE:-ghcr.io/thieso2/sandcastle-sandbox:latest}"
   124→  SANDCASTLE_USER="${SANDCASTLE_USER:-sandcastle}"
   125→  SANDCASTLE_GROUP="${SANDCASTLE_GROUP:-$SANDCASTLE_USER}"
   126→  SANDCASTLE_UID="${SANDCASTLE_UID:-220568}"
   127→  SANDCASTLE_GID="${SANDCASTLE_GID:-220568}"
   128→  SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT="${SANDCASTLE_HTTP_PORT:-80}"
   129→  SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT="${SANDCASTLE_HTTPS_PORT:-443}"
   130→  TCP_PORT_MIN="${SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MIN:-3000}"
   131→  TCP_PORT_MAX="${SANDCASTLE_TCP_PORT_MAX:-3099}"
   132→  SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE="${SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE:-selfsigned}"
   133→  SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER="${SANDCASTLE_ADMIN_USER:-admin}"
   134→
   135→  DOCKYARD_ROOT="${DOCKYARD_ROOT:-$SANDCASTLE_HOME/dockyard}"
   136→  DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX="${DOCKYARD_DOCKER_PREFIX:-sc_}"
   137→
   138→  # Single RFC 1918 /16 from which all Sandcastle Docker networks are carved.
   139→  # Must be a private range (10.x.x.x, 172.16-31.x.x, or 192.168.x.x).
   140→  SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET="${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET:-10.89.0.0/16}"
   141→  _priv_base="${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET%%/*}"        # e.g. "10.89.0.0"
   142→  _priv_prefix="${_priv_base%.*.*}"                  # e.g. "10.89"
   143→  DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR="${DOCKYARD_BRIDGE_CIDR:-${_priv_prefix}.0.1/24}"
   144→  DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR="${DOCKYARD_FIXED_CIDR:-${_priv_prefix}.0.0/24}"
   145→  DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE="${DOCKYARD_POOL_BASE:-${SANDCASTLE_PRIVATE_NET}}"
   146→  DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE="${DOCKYARD_POOL_SIZE:-24}"
   147→
   148→  DOCKER_SOCK="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/run/docker.sock"
   149→  DOCKER="${DOCKYARD_ROOT}/bin/docker"
   150→

    vnc_url(sandbox)
  end

class Sandbox < ApplicationRecord
  include ActionView::RecordIdentifier  # For dom_id in Turbo broadcasts

  belongs_to :user
  has_many :routes, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :container_metrics, dependent: :delete_all

  VNC_GEOMETRIES = %w[1280x900 1366x768 1440x900 1600x900 1920x1080 2560x1440].freeze
  VNC_DEPTHS = [ 8, 16, 24, 32 ].freeze

  validates :name, presence: true,
    uniqueness: { scope: :user_id, conditions: -> { where.not(status: %w[destroyed archived]) } }
  validates :name, format: { with: /\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{0,62}\z/, message: "must be lowercase alphanumeric" },
    unless: -> { status.in?(%w[destroyed archived]) }
  validates :status, inclusion: { in: %w[pending running stopped destroyed archived] }
  validates :image, presence: true
  validates :vnc_geometry, inclusion: { in: VNC_GEOMETRIES }
  validates :vnc_depth, inclusion: { in: VNC_DEPTHS }

  scope :active, -> { where.not(status: %w[destroyed archived]) }
  scope :archived, -> { where(status: "archived") }
  scope :running, -> { where(status: "running") }

  # Turbo Streams for real-time UI updates
  after_create_commit :broadcast_prepend_to_dashboard
  after_update_commit :broadcast_replace_to_dashboard
  after_destroy_commit :broadcast_remove_from_dashboard

  def full_name
    "#{user.name}-#{name}"
  end

  def connect_command
    "sandcastle connect #{name}"
  end

  def routed?
    routes.loaded? ? routes.any? : routes.exists?
  end

  def temp?
    temporary?
  end

  # Job lifecycle management
  def job_in_progress?
    job_status.present?
  end

  def job_failed?
    job_error.present?
